title: Ali Tarrak
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image= |name=Ali Tarrak |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]]''[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter: Defender of the Empire]]'' |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Nami]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Imperials, stay back or your precious scientist dies!''"
"''Then you will die as well, Tarrak! Continue, Mu 1.|Ali Tarrak and Thrawn|audio=AliTarrak-TF.flac|}} '''Ali Tarrak''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Nami]] who was the leader of her people during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Operating as [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], the Nami [[Ambush on Hashim|captured]] a prototype of the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] around [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]]. After a subsequent [[Battle of Kiilimaar|raid]] to abduct the TIE Defender project's [[Unidentified TIE Defender chief scientist|lead scientist]], Tarrak was able to begin producing her own TIE Defenders at a [[Tarrak (Deep Space Manufacturing Facility)|manufacturing facility]] in the region of the [[Eva-T system/Legends|Eva-T system]]. Tarrak arranged to supply TIE Defenders to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], but Imperial forces under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] were able to seize the first shipment before it reached the Rebels. Thrawn launched an [[Capture of Ali Tarrak|assault]] on Tarrak's TIE Defender factory, destroying the facility and ending the threat of the TIE Defender technology spreading. Tarrak was captured while trying to escape and, having impressed Thrawn, was offered the opportunity to serve [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. ==Biography== ===Crime lord=== {{Quote|This "crime lord" Tarrak is no ordinary pirate. She is the last leader of a nomadic warrior people known as the Nami.|Priest||audio=PriestTarrak-TF.flac}} Ali Tarrak was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] member of the [[Nami]], a once-powerful nomadic warrior group''[[TIE Fighter Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' who were driven into the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' and largely forgotten about by the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. By [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]]Tarrak's involvement in [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] follows shortly after [[Demetrius Zaarin]]'s failed [[Battle of Imperial Center|coup]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]]. the surviving Nami were operating as [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in the Outer Rim, with Tarrak as their leader and [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]]. Under Tarrak's leadership, the group were well trained and well equipped with [[R60 T-wing interceptor|T-wing]] and [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], and at least two [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]]. ===Fight for the TIE Defender=== {{Quote|Their ability to exploit the TIE Defender technology is probably minimal without the expertise to actually build more. However, they are probably aware of the Rneekii ransom offer and it is likely that they will make an attempt to capture the TIE Defender scientist. If this comes to pass, the Emperor wishes that Tarrak be taken prisoner.|Priest, on the Nami||audio=PriestNamiDefenders-TF.flac}} [[File:TIE_Defender.png|thumb|right|240px|Tarrak sought to acquire TIE Defender technology from the Empire.]] In 3.5 ABY [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]] attempted a [[Battle of Imperial Center|coup]] against [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Thwarted by loyalist forces, Zaarin began a [[Zaarin insurrection|campaign]] against the Empire, and hoped to secure a technological advantage over the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] by gaining sole control of the new [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]], one of the most advanced starfighters ever developed.''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' The Empire [[Evacuation of Research|evacuated]] the TIE Defender prototypes and key personnel from the [[Research|facility]] where the starfighter had been developed, but Tarrak saw the perfect opportunity to steal a specimen while the [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] was en route to [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]. Tarrak's raid piggybacked off the actions of a separate group of pirates, the [[Rneekii]], who were able to [[Ambush on Hashim|snare]] the Imperials in an [[Mine Type B|ion cannon minefield]] as they emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. The pirates captured the [[G'nabgib|shuttle]] carrying the TIE Defender project's [[Unidentified TIE Defender chief scientist|lead scientist]] before being driven off, and the prototypes were left vulnerable aboard the disabled [[CR92a Assassin-class corvette|modified corvette]] group [[Hashim group|''Hashim'']]. Tarrak dispatched Nami raiders led by the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] ''[[Dhoora]]'' to capture one of the prototypes. The Nami were not the only group seeking to take advantage of the situation and arrived to find the Imperial forces already engaged with starfighters from the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. With both sides distracted, the Nami boarded and captured one of the corvettes carrying a single TIE Defender unit, escaping into hyperspace before the Imperial defenders could respond. ===Capture of the scientist=== {{Quote|Tarrak's forces have procured TIE Defenders, or worse yet, have set up production facilities of their own for building the TIE Defender.|Imperial briefing officer||audio=ImperialOfficerTarrak-TF.flac}} The Empire was concerned that Tarrak may attempt to use the TIE Defender technology to regain her people's lost territory, but considered the threat to be low as the Nami lacked the expertise to produce more of the starfighters. Nevertheless, Tarrak's actions had brought her to the attention of the Emperor, who issued instructions for her to be taken [[Prison/Legends|prisoner]]. Tarrak soon learned that the Rneekii were to [[Battle of Kiilimaar|ransom]] the captive lead [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] to the Empire near [[Kiilimaar]] and devised a plan to capture the scientist during the exchange. [[File:Tarrak-TF.png|thumb|left|210px|Tarrak's TIE Defender manufacturing facility]] The Imperials had no intention of allowing the Rneekii to profit from their actions. As soon as the scientist was back in Imperial hands, the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruiser]] ''[[Red Claw]]'' entered the [[Kiilimaar system/Legends|Kiilimaar system]] to trap the pirates. While the Imperial forces were occupied with the Rneekii, the Nami YT-1300 light freighter ''[[Choora]]'' entered the [[Star system/Legends|system]] under T-wing and Y-wing escort. The Imperials were unable to prevent the ''Choora'' disabling and boarding the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] carrying the scientist, but as the ''Choora'' attempted to escape with its prize it was disabled by the [[TIE pilot|Imperial pilot]] [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]] and captured by the Empire. Several Nami prisoners were taken for interrogation in the hopes of locating Tarrak's base. Although Tarrak's attempt to capture the scientist had failed, her surprise attack had resulted in damage to the ''Red Claw''{{'s}} [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], leaving it stranded in the system. A [[Chemeti 1|corvette]] was dispatched to take the scientist to safety but an [[Chemeti's impostor|imposter]] with a fake [[Transponder code/Legends|transponder code]] arrived first and picked up the scientist while the ''Red Claw'' was fending off an attack by Zaarin's forces. The Imperials concluded that Zaarin was behind the deception, using the attack as a distraction. Whoever was behind the scientist's latest abduction, he soon ended up once more in Tarrak's custody. Using a [[Tarrak (Deep Space Manufacturing Facility)|manufacturing plant]] in the vicinity of the [[Eva-T system/Legends|Eva-T system]], Tarrak quickly began constructing TIE Defenders. Hoping to sell a great portion of the starfighters to the Rebel Alliance, Tarrak arranged for the Rebels to be present as observers while the Nami [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class medium cruiser]] ''[[Bognog]]'' and a [[Boggie Squadron|squadron]] of TIE Defenders [[Battle of Yrrna|assaulted]] an Imperial cargo transfer area in the [[Yrrna system/Legends|Yrrna system]]. ===The Empire strikes back=== {{Quote|You enabled the capture of the Nami leader Tarrak. The Emperor will be very pleased. He is looking forward to meeting his new servant.|Priest, to Maarek Stele||audio=PriestTarrakCaptured-TF.flac}} Both the Emperor and Thrawn were deeply concerned over the prospect of TIE Defenders ending up in Rebel hands. In order to mitigate the risk, Thrawn had been secretly overseeing the development of a new Imperial assault craft, the [[Missile Boat]], which was specifically developed to counter the TIE Defender. The additional risk that Tarrak may have been using the facility as the first target for a new campaign against the Empire, served only to hasten the deployment of the new weapon. Shortly after the Yrrna attack, Tarrak dispatched the first shipment of eight TIE Defenders to the Rebels in the ''Strike''-class cruiser ''[[Nuance]]'', but [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] learned of the planned rendezvous and Thrawn's forces captured the ''Nuance'' before it could deliver its cargo. Interrogation of the ''Nuance''{{'s}} crew revealed the location of Tarrak's manufacturing plant and Thrawn planned to personally oversee its destruction. Admiral Thrawn's Star Destroyer, the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Grey Wolf]]'', emerged from hyperspace a short distance from the manufacturing plant and launched its starfighters to engage the T-wings and Y-wings defending the facility. Finding Tarrak's TIE Defenders from [[Blue Devil Squadron|Blue Devil]] and [[Red Devil Squadron|Red Devil]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] powered down, Stele, piloting a lone Missile Boat, moved quickly to destroy them before they could be brought online, then eliminated the [[Mine Type C|minefield]] protecting the station, clearing the way for [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] to destroy the facility. [[File:AliTarrakFlees-TF.png|thumb|right|260px|Tarrak flees her TIE Defender manufacturing facility aboard the ''Dhoora''.]] When the factory's shields fell, Tarrak attempted to flee, leaving the station aboard the ''Dhoora'' with the lead scientist as a hostage. Stele, under orders to capture Tarrak alive, opened fire on the fleeing transport in an attempt to bring its [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] down. Tarrak threatened to [[Death/Legends|kill]] her hostage if the Imperial pilot did not cease his attack, but Thrawn called her bluff, assuring Tarrak that such an action would guarantee her own death. When the [[Transport/Legends|transport's]] shields fell, Stele used his [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] to prevent Tarrak's escape until Imperial [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing|Assault Gunboats]] arrived and disabled it with their [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]]. Despite the arrival of Rebel reinforcements, the ''Dhoora'' was captured and Tarrak taken into custody. Tarrak's defeat ended the threat of TIE Defender proliferation. Impressed by Tarrak, Thrawn convinced Emperor Palpatine of her potential, and the Emperor decided to offer Tarrak the opportunity to serve him. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Tarrak has attracted the interest of Admiral Thrawn. He sees in her a resemblance to himself. Having convinced the Emperor of her value, he has been directed to capture her alive. She is going to be offered the opportunity to serve the Emperor as Thrawn does.|Priest||audio=PriestTarrakThrawn-TF.flac}} [[File:AliTarrakCapture-TF.png|thumb|left|280px|Maarek Stele uses his Missile Boat's tractor beam to prevent Ali Tarrak's escape.]] Tarrak was a competent leader who ensured that the Nami were well equipped and who inspired loyalty in her followers. Although the Nami were relatively obscure pirates early in her tenure, Tarrak's attempts to seize control of the TIE Defender technology displayed both ambition and a willingness to take bold actions, even at the risk of incurring the wrath of the Empire, who viewed her actions a potential attempt to reclaim the Nami's former territory. She was quick to take advantage of the opportunity to steal a TIE Defender, and was cunning enough to utilize the Rneekii pirates as a distraction when making her own move. Tarrak's actions made an impression on Admiral Thrawn, who recognized some of his own traits in her. Thrawn was ultimately able to convince Emperor Palpatine that Tarrak would make a useful servant of the Empire. ==Behind the scenes== Ali Tarrak was created for ''[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter: Defender of the Empire]]'', the [[1994]] expansion pack for the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]. The character has only one line of dialogue, during the mission "Eliminate TIE Defender Factory", and the voice actor is not credited. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter: Defender of the Empire]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[TIE Fighter: Defender of the Empire: Official Secrets & Solutions]]'' *''[[TIE Fighter Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tarrak, Ali}} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified species]] [[Category:Pirates]]
